description_long: This sunshade features a radial design with eight slender, evenly spaced metal arms extending outward from a central hub, forming a symmetrical starburst pattern. The central hub appears to be a circular, metallic component, possibly with a decorative or functional cap. The arms are straight and uniform in thickness, suggesting a lightweight yet sturdy construction, likely intended to support a fabric or mesh canopy that is not currently visible. The overall structure is minimalistic and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or edge detailing. The sunshade is likely designed for outdoor use in urban environments such as parks, patios, or sidewalk cafes, providing shade when fully assembled with its canopy. The metallic finish gives it a modern, industrial appearance suitable for contemporary public spaces.
description: A modern, radial sunshade frame with eight straight metal arms extending from a central circular hub, designed for outdoor urban use. The structure is minimalistic and metallic, lacking visible canopy or decorative edge details.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full radial symmetry of the eight metal arms and the central hub.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the arms and the central hub, with arms fanning out in a single plane.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view mirroring the top, highlighting the central hub and the even spacing of the arms.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the arms and the central hub's profile.
attribute_canopy_design: radial, multi-arm frame, intended for circular canopy attachment, no visible fabric
attribute_support_structure_style: eight straight, evenly spaced metal arms radiating from a central hub, minimalist design
